stat_of_week: .636 OBP - James Wood posted a .636 OBP for Athletic Park Dragons this week, the highest single-week OBP mark in league history, obliterating the previous record of .414 set by General Hospital in Week 1
thriller_teams: Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs vs. Albuquerque Bombers
thriller_note: The Wonderdogs survived on the strength of dominant pitching — 62 Ks, 1.13 WHIP, and 7 NSVH — despite being badly outgunned at the plate, and this narrow escape keeps them relevant while the Bombers' inability to win close games is becoming an early-season pattern.
key_storyline: James Wood and Willson Contreras turned Athletic Park Dragons into the most terrifying offense in the league this week, combining for 11 runs, 6 HR, 19 RBI, and eye-popping OBP numbers — Wood's .636 and Contreras's .594 are historically absurd marks for a single week. The Dragons' 40 runs scored and .412 team OBP weren't just good, they were historic, and they did it convincingly enough to win 6–3 even with below-average pitching. If this duo keeps producing like this, Athletic Park is a legitimate title contender that nobody wants to face come playoff time.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers posted the league's worst pitching week — a 5.53 ERA, 34 Ks in only 42.1 IP, and zero dominant outings — yet somehow parlayed it into a 6–3 win, carrying the largest positive luck delta in the league at +0.91.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs delivered genuinely elite pitching all week — 62 Ks, a 4.30 ERA, 1.13 WHIP, 5 QS, and 7 NSVH — but their anemic offense cost them enough categories that their expected wins of 1.00 far outpaced their actual 0.4, the worst luck delta in the league at -0.60.
records_broken: James Wood's .636 OBP shattered the previous league record of .414 set by General Hospital in Week 1; Willson Contreras's .594 OBP would have been the new record on its own in any prior week.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Back-to-back elite weeks; 8 wins, 2.11 ERA, and an offense that scores from every position — nobody is touching this roster right now | same
2. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood's .636 OBP and Contreras's .594 are not normal — 40 runs and .412 team OBP is a historic statement week; this team is ascending fast | up
3. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's 5 HR explosion last week proved it wasn't a fluke; consistent top-5 category performer across all three weeks with solid pitching to match | same
4. Hiroshima Radiation | An 8–2 demolition of Canberra with 64 hits, league-leading 6 QS, and Bryce Harper's .552 OBP signals this team has fully woken up after a shaky Week 2 | up
5. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | A 1.65 ERA and 1.04 WHIP in a loss is the definition of unlucky — this pitching staff is elite and once the offense clicks, watch out | down
6. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Strongest pitching in the league over three weeks may finally be enough to climb; offense remains a structural crisis that one good waiver week won't fix | up
7. North Correa | Chris Sale's 22 Ks and Oneil Cruz's 6 SB show the ceiling is enormous, but 51 IP and a 4.73 ERA in a losing effort reveals the floor is still dangerous | down
8. Albuquerque Bombers | Three active adds including Josh Jung and Noelvi Marte show a manager trying to fix real problems; another 5–4 loss stings but the lineup is improving | up

9. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Merrill Kelly is a smart add and the pitching was creditable with 75 Ks, but an 8–2 blowout loss and zero NSVH points to a roster that needs more help than one wire move | same

10. General Hospital | The spotlight team this week — Kumar Rocker is a big swing and the 70 Ks in a tie show pitching upside, but a 5.48 ERA and a luck delta of -0.54 means they're genuinely underperforming expectations | down
11. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz is a weekly engine with 4 SB and 2 HR, but a 2.25 ERA somehow only produced a tie and the offense outside Elly remains thin | same
12. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6–3 again with zero quality starts and a 1.43 WHIP — Trevor Story's 9 RBI carried them last week and this roster cannot keep winning on individual heroics alone | down
13. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson hit 5 HR and they still lost 8–2; this team has pop but the pitching is consistently too soft to compete in a full-category format | same
14. Benchwarmers | The luck index doesn't lie — a +0.91 luck delta masks a roster with serious pitching problems; 34 Ks and a 5.53 ERA will eventually catch up to them | same
